name: DOEON KIM
headline: LLM Systems Engineer | Reliability, Evaluation, Runtime Delivery
summary: Builds production-minded AI systems with strongest public proof in tool-calling reliability, multimodal incident workflows, and reviewable runtime design.

Improved StagePilot benchmark success from 25% baseline to 90% on a 40-case expanded suite (13 edge cases including adversarial, unicode, oversized payloads) with parser recovery and bounded retry, then exposed the same runtime story through live review, OpenTelemetry traces, and regression-gate surfaces.
Extends the same reliability posture into bounded live reviewer APIs for incident review, governed analytics, document QA, air-gapped operations, and high-trust workflow delivery.

bullets: Delivered AI systems across RAG, incident operations, and real-time operator workflows under practitioner and Microsoft Korea review cycles.
Improved Haptix multi-label performance to AP 96.8 on single-tag and AP 85.0 on triple-tag cases while also building backend and RAG delivery surfaces for Honeypot and TwinCity UI.
Turned ambiguous project asks into reviewable runtime surfaces, demos, and handoff artifacts across multiple team systems.
Shipped customer-facing Node.js, Express, MongoDB, HTML/CSS/JavaScript product features and integrated OpenAI-powered chatbot workflows.
Collaborated asynchronously across US time zones to keep feature delivery stable across frontend, backend, and AI integration boundaries.

experience.role: Strategic Backbone Network & Security Team Lead
bullets: Led a 6-person squad in a 24/7 mission-critical environment with no-fail operational expectations.
Handled roughly 8-9 incidents per month and led triage, mitigation, IAM/access control, and emergency recovery.
Owned security readiness, configuration control, and high-trust handoff discipline across production operations.

projects.name: StagePilot
bullets: Built the canonical repo for parser middleware, StagePilot orchestration, and BenchLab eval tooling.
Improved 40-case expanded benchmark (13 edge cases: adversarial, unicode, oversized payloads) from 25% to 90% with middleware and bounded retry, with reviewable proof surfaces.
Added live-review, failure-taxonomy, trace-observability, regression-gate, and protocol-matrix APIs so parser drift, retries, and cross-protocol behavior stay inspectable.

projects.name: AegisOps
bullets: Turned logs and monitoring screenshots into structured RCA, action plans, and handoff artifacts.
Added JSON/schema guardrails, BYOK and no-key modes, plus postmortem-pack, escalation-readiness, and live-escalation-preview surfaces for reviewable incident handoff.

projects.name: Aegis-Air
bullets: Built a local-first incident review system for teams that cannot send production telemetry to public model APIs.
Returned structured RCA with severity, failure bucket, evidence, immediate actions, and offline deployment proof under deterministic fallback paths.

projects.name: Enterprise LLM Adoption Kit
bullets: Implemented RBAC, prompt-injection detection, PII redaction, audit logging, JSONL eval pipelines, and rollout artifacts.
Added rollout-gates and customer-architecture-pack surfaces so governance and deployment decisions stay reviewable.

projects.name: Applied Ops Archive
bullets: Applied reliability, approval-boundary, and handoff design to a high-stakes operational system with visible evidence and decision traces.

education: Korea National Open University | B.S. in Computer Science (In Progress, Entered Mar 2026)
certifications: AI-900, SnowPro Associate, Palantir Foundry Data Engineer Associate, Databricks Platform Architect (AWS/GCP), Datadog Observability
